title: putoria calabrica subsp. hispanica
intro: Putoria calabrica subsp. hispanica, also known as Spanish bird cherry, is a type of flowering plant in the Oleaceae family, native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Putoria calabrica subsp. hispanica include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its leaves and flowers, which can be used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures.
